title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Market Recap for Friday, May 29, 2026 (Last Trading Day)

items.headline: Wall Street Climbs to New Records, Driven by Tech Enthusiasm and AI Demand
items.summary: U.S. stock indexes concluded the week on a strong note, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite reaching new all-time highs. The Dow Jones Industrial Average surpassed 51,000 for the first time ever, fueled by robust performance in the technology sector, particularly companies benefiting from AI demand. Optimism surrounding a potential U.S.-Iran ceasefire also contributed to positive market sentiment, overshadowing persistent inflation concerns. This marks the S&P 500's seventh consecutive daily gain and ninth straight weekly advance.

items.headline: Dell Technologies Soars on Blowout Earnings and AI Demand
items.summary: Dell Technologies ($DELL) surged after reporting first-quarter earnings that significantly surpassed expectations, driven by robust demand for its AI servers. The company also raised its full-year guidance, further boosting investor confidence.

items.headline: NetApp Rallies Following Strong Earnings Report
items.summary: Data storage provider NetApp ($NTAP) saw significant gains after delivering a strong quarterly earnings report that highlighted robust growth.

items.headline: ServiceNow Jumps Amid Renewed Software Enthusiasm
items.summary: ServiceNow ($NOW) experienced a notable increase, benefiting from renewed enthusiasm in the software sector.

items.headline: Clorox Leads Consumer Staples Lower
items.summary: The Clorox Company ($CLX) was among the biggest decliners as investors rotated out of the defensive consumer staples sector into more growth-oriented areas of the market.

items.headline: Costco Wholesale Slides Despite Solid Q3 Earnings
items.summary: Costco Wholesale ($COST) shares fell despite reporting solid Q3 earnings, as broader consumer defensive stocks experienced declines.

items.headline: Autodesk Drops on Acquisition News
items.summary: Autodesk ($ADSK) saw its shares fall after announcing a $3.6 billion all-cash acquisition of MaintainX, despite posting stronger-than-expected quarterly results.

items.headline: Technology Sector Drives Market to New Highs with AI Momentum
items.summary: The information technology sector was the top performer, gaining 1.87% on Friday. This surge was primarily fueled by continued enthusiasm for Artificial Intelligence (AI) and strong earnings reports from key tech companies.

items.headline: Hopes for US-Iran Ceasefire Deal Buoy Investor Sentiment
items.summary: Investor sentiment was positively influenced by reports suggesting a draft agreement for a 60-day ceasefire extension between the U.S. and Iran, easing fears of broader regional conflict and potential energy supply disruptions.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Data Signals Persistent Inflation and Consumer Strain
items.summary: Recent economic data showed an increase in initial jobless claims and a rise in both PCE inflation and core PCE inflation for April. While personal income remained unchanged, personal spending grew. Despite overall consumer spending growth, signs of financial strain are emerging among U.S. households due to elevated inflation and energy prices.

items.headline: Major Tech Companies Poised to Report Earnings Next Week
items.summary: The upcoming week is set to feature key earnings reports from several influential technology companies, including Broadcom ($AVGO), CrowdStrike ($CRWD), Hewlett Packard Enterprise ($HPE), and Palo Alto Networks ($PANW). Investors will be closely watching these reports for insights into the tech sector's continued performance and AI-driven growth.
